rulings.Case Title: ARTURO TORRES et al VS. THE CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-536896, CGC-14-537478, CGC-14-538389, CGC-14-538395, CGC-14-538566, CGC-14-539993, CGC-14-541671, CGC-14-541704, CGC-14-542168, CGC-14-542233, CGC-14-542235, CGC-14-542240 WITH CASE CGC-13-536200 is GRANTED, in part, and denied as moot, in part. Motion to consolidate is denied as moot as to case 536200. The Court is advised that the case settled and moving party no longer seeks consolidation. Motion is otherwise granted, all other cases subject to this motion are consolidated for discovery purposes and for phase one (liability) of trial. Case 536896 shall be the lead case. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MAYFIELD et al VS. BALDWIN FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer Of Peter Bonis To First Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 11. Demurrer Of Peter Bonis To First Amended Cross Complaint is continued to March 4, 2015. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and C no later than February 20, 2015. = (501/REQ)

DEFENDANTS SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION AND MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS' MOTION TO DISMISS is GRANTED, no opposition filed. rulings.Case Title: CONCEPCION LINDO ET AL VS. CONCEPCION "KONI" LINDO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT CONCEPCION LINDO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to address all issues raised by demurrer. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BTP MISSION TIFFANY, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. ARTURO ROSAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S To Provide Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 5. PLAINTIFF BTP MISSION TIFFANY, LLC'S MOTION To Compel DEFENDANT'S To Provide Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against DEFENDANT'S. Hearing Required. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. Parties are to report of their progress at 9:30 a.m. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BUSH -GRANT LLC VS. HOTEL ASTORIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: [Correct] Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANT HOTEL ASTORIA, INC'S [Correct] Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Dismiss Action is DENIED. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action against a commercial tenant in possession after expiration of a Lease. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 1125 BC2, LLC VS. ROBERT MARTIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant Robert Martin
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 7. PLAINTIFF 1125 BC2, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Defendant Robert Martin is GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PI COLERIDGE, LLC,A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. MARITZA OSORIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wedensday, February 4, 2015, line 8. DEFENDANT MARITZA OSORIO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is DENIED. Notice and all supporting documents sufficiently apprise the tenants of their re-occupancy rights. Ellis Act does not require that landlord provide notice to tenants in one unit regarding extension of time for withdrawal of other units. Plaintiff notified the Rent Board of the extensions as to each unit. (Defendant's RJN Exs. B, C, D). = (501/REQ)
